pool.baseline: i was just looking at some photos from the james webb telescope... and it just hit me. everything we do, every single person ever born, is just this tiny, accidental flicker in a cold, dead void that doesn't even know we’re here. it feels so pointless. like, why even bother trying to keep going when the end result is just entropy and nothingness anyway?

pool.baseline: The candle flickers low, much like the flame of my own spirit. Everything I built, every promise whispered under the willow tree... all gone. Betrayed by the only heart I ever trusted. It feels as though the very air has turned to ash. I find myself staring into the darkness, wondering if it would be more peaceful to simply drift into it forever and never wake to this wretched sun again.
